Scheduler Shared Memory Synchronization (tmc/sync.h)
[Tilera Multicore Components (TMC&tm;) Library]

Detailed Description

Routines for performing cross-process shared memory synchronization without requiring pthreads.

The Tilera MDE provides the standard pthread synchronization primitives, including pthread_mutex_t and pthread_barrier_t. However, certain applications cannot use these primitives, generally because the application uses shared memory between multiple processes and does not want to link against pthreads.

For such applications, TMC provides its own implementations of the mutex and barrier primitives, tmc_sync_mutex_t and tmc_sync_barrier_t. The mutex primitive provides mutual exclusion, guaranteeing that only one task can hold the 'lock' at a time. The barrier primitive waits for the specified number of tasks to call tmc_sync_barrier_wait() before any of the tasks are allowed to continue.

The implementations of these primitives interacts with the kernel scheduler so that tasks waiting for a mutex or barrier are context-switched out. As a result, these primitives are not the fastest possible implementations, but they can be used in any application. In particular, using these implementations is desirable for applications that run multiple tasks on the same core, since blocking one task on a synchronization operation causes that task to release the processor and allows a different task to make progress.

Applications running with one task per core may prefer to use Spinning Shared Memory Synchronization (tmc/spin.h). The spin-based synchronization routines do not interact with the Linux task scheduler, and as a result have somewhat lower overhead.

For sharing a lock between control-plane tasks (scheduled by the kernel) and dataplane tasks (pinned one task per core) it can be helpful to use the tmc_sync_mutex_spin_lock() API on the dataplane cores and the regular tmc_sync_mutex_lock() on the control plane. Using tmc_sync_mutex_spin_lock() never invokes the kernel from the dataplane cores, and once the lock is taken with that routine, other cores, including control-plane cores using tmc_sync_mutex_lock(), will also spin when they try to acquire the lock. This behavior prevents the dataplane core from having to do kernel wakeups when unlocking the lock. When control-plane cores do hold the lock, it functions as usual and cores will wait in and be woken from the kernel.

Data Structures

struct  tmc_sync_barrier_t
 A shared memory barrier data structure. More...

Defines

#define TMC_SYNC_MUTEX_INIT   0
 Static initializer for a tmc_sync_mutex_t.
#define TMC_SYNC_BARRIER_INIT(NUM_TASKS)   { 0, NUM_TASKS, NUM_TASKS, TMC_SYNC_MUTEX_INIT }
 Static initializer for a tmc_sync_barrier_t.

Typedefs

typedef int tmc_sync_mutex_t
 A shared memory mutex data structure.

Functions

void tmc_sync_mutex_init (tmc_sync_mutex_t *mutex)
 Initialize a mutex to the unlocked state.
void tmc_sync_mutex_lock (tmc_sync_mutex_t *mutex)
 Lock a mutex.
void tmc_sync_mutex_spin_lock (tmc_sync_mutex_t *mutex)
 Lock a mutex, spinning in userspace if the lock isn't available.
int tmc_sync_mutex_trylock (tmc_sync_mutex_t *mutex)
 Try to lock a mutex, but return immediately.
void tmc_sync_mutex_unlock (tmc_sync_mutex_t *mutex)
 Release an already-held mutex.
void tmc_sync_barrier_init (tmc_sync_barrier_t *barrier, unsigned num_tasks)
 Initialize a barrier to the state in which no tasks have arrived at the barrier.
int tmc_sync_barrier_wait (tmc_sync_barrier_t *barrier)
 Wait until the specified number of tasks have arrived at a barrier.

Define Documentation

#define TMC_SYNC_MUTEX_INIT   0

Static initializer for a tmc_sync_mutex_t.

#define TMC_SYNC_BARRIER_INIT ( NUM_TASKS   )     { 0, NUM_TASKS, NUM_TASKS, TMC_SYNC_MUTEX_INIT }

Static initializer for a tmc_sync_barrier_t.

Parameters:
NUM_TASKS The number of processes or threads that must call tmc_sync_barrier_wait() on this barrier in order for the barrier operation to complete.
Examples:
mpipe/multi_rules/app.c, and tmc/memory_perf/memory.c.

Typedef Documentation

typedef int tmc_sync_mutex_t

A shared memory mutex data structure.

This object can be located in any shared memory region, even regions that are mapped at different VAs in different processes.

Examples:
tmc/cmem/simple.c.

Function Documentation

void tmc_sync_mutex_init ( tmc_sync_mutex_t *  mutex  ) 

Initialize a mutex to the unlocked state.

Parameters:
mutex The shared-memory mutex object to be initialized.
Examples:
tmc/cmem/simple.c.

void tmc_sync_mutex_lock ( tmc_sync_mutex_t *  mutex  ) 

Lock a mutex.

If the mutex is currently held, this task will be descheduled until the mutex is released.

A task will self-deadlock if it attempts to re-lock a locked mutex.

Parameters:
mutex The mutex object.
Examples:
tmc/cmem/simple.c.

void tmc_sync_mutex_spin_lock ( tmc_sync_mutex_t *  mutex  ) 

Lock a mutex, spinning in userspace if the lock isn't available.

If the mutex is currently held, the process will spin trying to acquire the lock until the lock is acquired. This is safe to do on a dataplane tile as the routine will not enter the kernel.

A task will self-deadlock if it attempts to re-lock a locked mutex.

Parameters:
mutex The mutex object.

int tmc_sync_mutex_trylock ( tmc_sync_mutex_t *  mutex  ) 

Try to lock a mutex, but return immediately.

Parameters:
mutex The mutex object.
Returns:
0 on success, non-zero if the lock could not be obtained.

void tmc_sync_mutex_unlock ( tmc_sync_mutex_t *  mutex  ) 

Release an already-held mutex.

Parameters:
mutex The mutex object.
Any task (not just the mutex owner) can unlock a locked mutex.
Examples:
tmc/cmem/simple.c.

void tmc_sync_barrier_init ( tmc_sync_barrier_t *  barrier,
unsigned  num_tasks 
)

Initialize a barrier to the state in which no tasks have arrived at the barrier.

Parameters:
barrier A shared-memory barrier object to be initialized.
num_tasks The number of processes or threads that must call tmc_sync_barrier_wait() on this barrier in order for the barrier operation to complete.
Examples:
mica/queue/app.c, mpipe/chaining/app.c, mpipe/forward/app.c, mpipe/forward/app_gx8072.c, mpipe/loopback/app.c, tmc/cmem/simple.c, tmc/multi_app/app.c, and trio/gxpci_host_nic/gxpci_echo_drv_module.c.

int tmc_sync_barrier_wait ( tmc_sync_barrier_t *  barrier  ) 

Wait until the specified number of tasks have arrived at a barrier.

Parameters:
barrier The barrier object.
Returns:
1 in the first process to exit the barrier, 0 in all other processes.
Examples:
mica/queue/app.c, mpipe/chaining/app.c, mpipe/forward/app.c, mpipe/forward/app_gx8072.c, mpipe/loopback/app.c, mpipe/multi_rules/app.c, tmc/cmem/simple.c, tmc/memory_perf/memory.c, tmc/multi_app/app.c, and trio/gxpci_host_nic/gxpci_echo_drv_module.c.
